products.title: Destination Highlight: Big Nugget
products.description: Enter the Gold Rush Training Camp, to learn how to extract paydirt, sluice, and assay your results. Learn the art of gold panning with a humorous and educational show. Practice your technique in heated troughs. Authentic gold is guaranteed! Weigh your fortune with our assayers. Meet canine athletes that have run the world-famous Iditarod race as well as our experienced mushers. Hear about the history of this breed through personal stories and an amazing collection of the gear necessary on long-distance races like the Iditarod. Observe the musher hooking them up to an authentic sled for a fast practice lap around the track. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed! Take a guided tour of the 350-ton Sixty Mile Dredge – one of the last rescued authentic gold dredges used for placer mining. Take a break with lunch at the Alaskan BBQ where our menu is based on Alaskan home-grown foods and finish with a campfire complete with s'mores, or soda floats (weather dependent), lots of performances, and laughter with our costumed characters. You will return to the ship not as a cheechako (newcomer to Alaska) but as a seasoned sourdough (someone who has experienced Alaska and is now a part of its history and culture) with lots of memories to share back home.
highlights: Meet Iditarod dogs and their mushers, and learn about the role sled dogs played in Alaska’s history. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed!
Visit the gold dredge and uncover its inner workings.
Savor a BBQ buffet, enjoying smoked ribs, chicken, salmon chowder delicious sides and dessert on the riverside patios.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Best of Skagway
products.description:
Experience many of Skagway's highlights in one tour. First, climb aboard the White Pass and Yukon Railway and ascend into the scenic mountains, hearing stories of the miners who made this difficult, or once-treacherous, sojourn on foot more than a century ago. Arriving in Fraser, British Columbia board a motor coach to travel south through the pass where your driver/guide will point out sites including Tormented Valley and Pitchfork Falls. At the foot of the pass is Liarsville Gold Rush Trail Camp. Here a cast of sourdoughs and dance hall girls will entertain you with a humorous melodrama, recite a poem by the "Bard of the North" and teach you the art of finding gold! You are guaranteed to hit pay dirt and you get to keep the gold you find... and there is nothing like panning for gold to work up an appetite! At the Liarsville Cookhouse the grill is fired up for a delicious feast of wild Alaskan salmon, baked chicken, rice pilaf, a variety of salads, baked beans, cornbread and dessert. 
Your tour culminates at the world-famous Red Onion Saloon where you will be greeted by sumptuously dressed madams. Upstairs in the Bombay room, enjoy a champagne toast to the courageous ladies of 1898. A few more steps takes you to what was the most luxurious brothel of its day, now faithfully restored to its present glory. On your tour through the brothel museum, you will hear stories of the infamous madams who worked there, and see the prized silver dress and other artifacts left behind by the "soiled doves". Embrace the spirit of '98 and enjoy the experience of a lifetime!
highlights: Travel the White Pass and Yukon Railway, following the footsteps of miners who travelled into the mountains on foot.
Discover the incredible beauty of British Columbia's Tormented Valley and Pitchfork Falls.
Sip a glass of champagne at the famous Red Onion Saloon.
Savor a delicious meal of wild Alaskan salmon, baked chicken, rice pilaf, salads, baked beans, cornbread, and dessert.
Immerse yourself in gold rush life at the Gold Rush Trail Camp where you'll learn how to pan for gold.

products.description: Embark on a leisurely sightseeing drive that provides a captivating look at Victoria, while you relax in complete comfort. products.title: Small Group: Exclusive Flightseeing and Taste of Alaska
products.description: Indulge in the perfect blend of Ketchikan's finest offerings, as you embark on an unforgettable journey combining spectacular scenery and mountain top flightseeing with a luxurious and authentic Alaskan seafood feast at a secluded world-famous George Inlet Lodge.
highlights: Discover firsthand why Alaska is affectionately known as “The Last Frontier." This extraordinary excursion promises a once-in-a-lifetime experience that will undoubtedly be the highlight of your Alaskan getaway.
Feel the exhilaration as a DeHavilland Beaver aircraft roars to life, with your skilled pilot at the helm, as you ascend from the sparkling ocean below.
Enjoy your pilot’s narration and details of the area with individual headsets and a guaranteed window seat.
Glide gently over the Tongass Narrows, taking in the bustling communities below and marveling at the awe-inspiring mountain ranges that define this remarkable corner of the world.

Your flight to George Inlet Lodge will transport you over rugged peaks at the southern tip of Revillagigedo Island. En route, keen observers might spot mountain goats resting on pristine snowpack or black bears foraging for succulent berries, or humpback whales breaching in the ocean.

As your floatplane gracefully touches down on the tranquil waters of George Inlet, you will arrive at the lodge where your host welcomes you. Have your cameras ready to capture the breathtaking sight of your floatplane taking off, framed by the magnificent Tongass National Forest.

Indulge in a mouthwatering Alaskan Tasting Adventure featuring your choice of three craft beers or Ciders from renowned Alaska breweries and Cidery, each carefully paired with a selection of delectable dishes.

Start with our homemade George Inlet Lodge crab chowder (or opt for a fresh salad with an Asian-style dressing). Savor the flavors of our fresh crab egg rolls and a crab cake made according to our lodge recipe, followed by a half steaming hot Dungeness crab, cooked to perfection.

Conclude your meal with a delightful piece of blueberry-topped cheesecake, a perfect blend of sweetness and tartness. As you savor each bite, you'll be entertained with a lesson in the art of crab cracking, hear entertaining stories about Alaskan Fishing and Crabbing as well as a brief history of the lodge.

Conclude your tour experience with a beautiful 25-minute coastal drive that provides a glimpse into our vibrant waterfront community and its local history, charming harbors, fascinating totem parks, and the authentic Alaskan way of life.

products.description:
Explore Victoria, renowned as Canada’s “City of Gardens” on this scenic drive through the historic downtown and visit to one of the world’s most recognized gardens—the breathtaking Butchart Gardens. Stroll the Sunken Garden, the Rose Garden, the Japanese Garden, Star Pond, the Italian Garden, and the Mediterranean Garden. products.title: Small Group: Scenic River Float through Bald Eagle Preserve
products.description: Discover the natural wonders and majestic wildlife of Alaska on a rafting exploration that glides you through the Chilkat Bald Eagle Preserve. Your expedition begins aboard a jet-powered catamaran that will take you through the waterways of Lynn Fjord to the town of Haines. A short, but panoramic, drive down the National Scenic Byway leads to the launch point for your river rafting journey. After a quick safety briefing, climb into an 18-foot inflatable raft and set off down the Chilkat River. Glide through the protected wilderness and keep watch for the park's famed birds of prey soaring overhead. You might also spot moose, or even bear roaming the riverbanks. Go ashore for a simple lunch and homemade cookies enjoyed against the most majestic backdrop imaginable. Follow the river’s edge back to Haines before the brisk catamaran ride back to Skagway.
highlights: Witness the majesty of Alaska on an expedition that travels by land, by sea, and by river.
Board a jet-powered catamaran and cruise through Lynn Fjord to the town of Haines, Alaska.

products.title: Destination Highlight: A Misty Fjords Flightseeing
products.description: Experience a bird's-eye view of awe-inspiring Misty Fjords National Monument on a floatplane flightseeing tour that soars above this unbridled terrain. You’ll lift off from a calm body of water and listen to commentary from an expert guide using the plane's headsets. Every guest is guaranteed a window seat, so you won't miss a thing. Look out onto a landscape of cascading waterfalls, alpine lakes, dense forests, snow-capped peaks, and 3,000-foot granite cliffs. products.description: Go ashore at the historic George Inlet Lodge for an all-out Alaskan crab feast. Chart a course for majestic beauty, outdoor adventure, and an incredible meal in the Alaskan wilds. Begin with a coastal drive that passes historic saw mills, waterfalls, and Native Alaskan totem poles. Board a sightseeing vessel and glide in the shadows of snow-capped mountains. View the Mahoney Glacial Cirque and 2,000-foot waterfalls. Keep watch for seals and otters playing in their natural habitat. As the Captain pulls up to the first crab pot, get ready to lend a hand. Join the crew and help haul in the soaked pots to see if there are Dungeness beauties within. After checking the pots, make way to the historic George Inlet Lodge for an authentic Alaskan crab feast. Savor the day’s catch, served with clarified butter and salad with Asian-style dressing. Be sure to leave room for dessert – cheesecake drenched in Alaskan blueberries. During Lunch, try your luck in a customary shell stacking contest. May the best stacker win.

products.title: Small Group: Chilkoot Trail Hiking and Rafting
products.description: The Canadian Gold Rush Trail, also known as the Chilkoot Trail, was the most direct route prospectors could take to reach the Yukon River during the Klondike Gold Rush. Depart Skagway, once a busy tent city filled with fortune hunters dreaming of untold wealth, and follow in the prospectors' footsteps as you hike the first two gentle miles of their journey. Drink in the beauty and tranquility of the lush rainforest, listen to birdsong and marvel at colorful wildflowers as the trail ascends to Saintly Hill, where gorgeous views await. At the scenic Taiya River, board an 18-foot craft for the peaceful float (no whitewater) to the tidal estuary that offers spectacular views of fjords, snow-capped peaks, hanging glaciers, and fields of flowers. This tour will expand your understanding of this region's dramatic history, even as you marvel at its peaceful stillness today.
highlights: Hike the first portion of the historic Chilkoot Trail, the centerpiece of the Klondike Gold Rush International Historical Park.
Float between majestic snow-capped peaks to the tidal estuary, where the forest gives way to fields of beach greens and wild iris.
Delve into the area's fascinating history as you follow your guide through the lush rainforest, observing wildflowers, colorful birds, and all manner of wildlife.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Tongass Rainforest Trail Expedition
products.description: Take a walk on the wild side — literally — in Tongass National Forest, and marvel at the ferocious beauty of the Alaskan wilderness. At 500 square miles (the size of West Virginia), ‘the Tongass' is the world's largest temperate rain forest, home to majestic mountains, verdant old-growth forest, fjords, bays, islands and streams. Unspoiled natural surroundings are center stage in this tour. Your small group is met on the dock by a seasoned guide (who will accompany you throughout) for the short drive to the trailhead. Step into the ancient forest, fill your lungs with clean, cool air and allow peace and solitude to envelope you. During the three-mile walk, there will be pauses for photos and wildlife sightings. Listen and watch as your experienced guide shares secrets of the forest and leads you to the best viewing spots. During the salmon-spawning season (May through October) you may see enormous bears scoop fish from rushing streams. The very essence of the Tongass is its towering tree canopy, dominated by evergreens, red and yellow cedar, hemlock, and spruce. Here, nearly every intersection is an animal crossing. Birdsong is the lingua franca. Come to this primal place to regain your sense of awe and childlike wonder.
highlights: Witness nature's creatures — eagles on the wing, spawning salmon, black bear— in their own habitat.
Behold the splendor and magnificence of America's largest national forest, more than 17 million acres of exquisite terrain.
Drink in the forest's sweet clean air and allow its ancient stillness to refresh your body and soul.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Gold Fever and Alaskan Sled Dogs
products.description: Your adventure begins when you disembark and meet your costumed guide who “lives” in 1898 and is there to accompany you on your Alaskan experience. Your tour includes an amazing encounter with sled dogs and mushers, and a heaping dose of gold fever. Your ticket includes everything you need for a trip to the Klondike. Enter the Gold Rush Training Camp, to learn how to extract paydirt, sluice, and assay your results. Learn the art of gold panning with a humorous and educational show. Practice your technique in heated water troughs. Authentic gold is guaranteed! Weigh your fortune with our assayers. Meet canine athletes that have run the world-famous Iditarod race as well as experienced mushers. Hear about the history of this breed through personal stories and an amazing collection of all the gear necessary on long-distance races like the Iditarod. Observe the musher hooking them up to an authentic sled for a fast practice lap around the track. The next generation of puppies who are there for you to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed! Return to the ship not as a cheechako (newcomer to Alaska) but as a seasoned sourdough (someone who has experienced Alaska and is now a part of its history and culture) with lots of memories to share back home.
highlights: Meet Iditarod dogs and their mushers, and learn about the role sled dogs played in Alaska’s history. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed!

products.description:
A two-hour sightseeing electric-assisted bicycle ride through Victoria's remarkable streetscapes offers a unique view of the city, as you pedal through Beacon Hill Park along Dallas Road Waterfront Trail through Chinatown and into the Inner Harbour. Meet up with your team of professional rider guides for a quick safety briefing. products.title: Destination Highlight: Victoria Highlights and History Walking Tour
products.description: Walk with a knowledgeable local guide to learn about Victoria's scenic, cultural, architectural, and historical highlights on a tour that includes city attractions that can only be reached on foot.
highlights: Meet your guide at the pier and set out to discover Victoria on foot, a tour that’s sure to fascinate and entertain. Along the way, stroll along the scenic oceanfront through historic James Bay, the oldest residential neighborhood in the city. From here, continue to the breathtaking downtown Inner Harbor, a bustling commercial core with regular seaplane departures to the surrounding islands, street performers, local crafts, and food vendors.

The Inner Harbor is also home to the Royal BC Museum, BC Legislative Buildings, and iconic Fairmont Empress Hotel. Walk to Victoria's stunning,19th-century Parliament Buildings, home to British Columbian government since 1897, and get up close in the walk-only areas that take you to the front entrance of these neoclassical gem. Arrive at Thunderbird Park to see its towering totem poles and ornate aboriginal art before continuing to the University of Victoria campus and stopping at the Aboriginal Long House, built in the traditional Coast Salish style. During your visit, your guide shares tall stories, the history of the area, and an insider’s knowledge of the city of Victoria. Following your tour, you may continue with your group for the guided walk back to the pier via the James Bay neighborhood or opt to remain in the downtown area for shopping and exploration, and return to the pier at your leisure.

products.title: Small Group: Ghost and Goodtime Girls Walking Tour
products.description:
Join a madam from the Red Onion Saloon for a fun and informative walking tour through downtown Skagway!
First, you’ll be greeted at the pier by a lavishly costumed and highly entertaining Madam. After a quick shuttle transfer to town, she will guide you through the back alleys and former red-light districts of Skagway. Here, women of the evening brushed shoulders with notorious characters, wide-eyed stampeders, and dangerous odds. Stories of Klondike Kate, Cad Wilson and Diamond Lil depict the adventurous stories of the women who stampeded north. You will pass historic and haunted gold rush buildings as you learn of Skagway’s rich history and the women who helped make it what it is.
Your experience culminates with a stop at the world-famous Red Onion Saloon’s restored Brothel Museum. Here, join in a champagne toast and explore the hallways and bedrooms of this celebrated brothel, observing original relics and images of the ladies who worked there. Bring your camera and you may even catch a glimpse of the resident ghost, Lydia!
*Your tour concludes at the Red Onion Saloon and guests may choose to stay in town and explore more of Skagway or take the short walk back to the pier.
highlights: Visit the Red Onion Saloon and Brothel Museum and see the artifacts left behind by the working women over 125 years ago.
Explore Skagway's back streets and be entertained by your masterful guide and Madam on this walking tour that's been featured on podcasts, ghost hunting shows, and even the Discovery channel.

products.title: Small Group: Kitchen Science Alaska Garden to Table Food Tour
products.description: Experience a piece of American history – and cuisine - with a visit to the historic Skagway Inn, where you mingle with local Alaskans, immerse yourself in centuries of tradition and discover the culinary secrets of this charming rural community.
highlights: After a brief tour through the city of Skagway, meet with the chef and owner of the historic Skagway Inn and embark on a behind-the-scenes tour of the facilities, as you discover the science that transforms garden-grown ingredients into delicious meals.

Stroll through the inn’s culinary garden and learn about the foods and herbs native to Alaska, along with the techniques used to catch and smoke fresh local fish. Then, head inside the inn, which once served as a brothel during the Klondike Gold Rush.

Here, learn the science behind making a loaf of sourdough bread, as you feed the establishment’s sourdough starter and savor some organic veggies, before transforming simple ingredients from the garden into a sweet concoction – your very own homemade ice cream.

Finish your culinary journey with a hint of luxury, as you enjoy a golden, non-alcoholic drink - a tasty reminder of the town's gold rush past - before returning to your awaiting ship with a few new recipes and skills to bring back home.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Historic Ketchikan Trolley
products.description: Step aboard a festive trolley and be entertained by your guide’s engaging stories about Ketchikan, renowned as “Alaska’s First City” and the “Salmon Capital of the World.” As you traverse narrow downtown streets, you'll pass by colorful landmarks, bustling boat harbors and the scenic Ketchikan Creek. Your driver guide will share stories that provide a fascinating glimpse into the city's past and present, with special attention to its historical significance and lively atmosphere. Your first stop is the Alaska Rainforest Sanctuary. Discover the beauty of Alaska’s temperate rainforest on this 40-acre private reserve, offering an up-close look at the landscape, wildlife, and culture that make this region so special. Begin with a private stop at Herring Cove, where you might spot harbor seals, bald eagles, and even black bears from a scenic viewing platform. Wrap up your visit with time at the General Store to browse for souvenirs and enjoy a drink at the cozy coffee shop or bar. This relaxed and enriching tour blends natural wonders, wildlife, and Native heritage for a truly memorable experience. Once reboarding the trolley, you have the option of returning to the ship or being dropped off in downtown Ketchikan to explore on your own and return to the ship at your leisure.

products.title: Small Group: Mendenhall Glacier Canoe Paddle and Trek
products.description: Experience the consistent and stunning Alaskan views from both land and water on your journey to the awe-inspiring Mendenhall Glacier. Mendenhall Lake is a treasure trove of natural beauty that rests in front of the glacier. Your guide will outfit you with gear, orient you to your canoe and provide a safety briefing before you paddle toward the Mendenhall Glacier. Glide between the towering cliffs and catch a glimpse of some of Alaska's amazing wildlife. Look for white, small, and slender Arctic Tern seabird nest near the lake. Mountain goats appear high in the mountains along the rocky outcroppings. Watch as billies, nannies and kids make their way to the Rock Peninsula jutting out into the lake. Black bears are also seen occasionally in the brush. As you paddle across Mendenhall Lake, you'll pass floating blue-hued icebergs and the powerful Nugget Falls waterfall cascading down the mountainside. When you reach land, trade your paddling gear for backpacks and trekking poles and absorb the beauty of the beautiful thirteen-mile long river of ice. After landing the canoe at the glacier beach, trade out the paddles for trekking poles and begin the hike over the rocky terrain carved out by the glacier. You will get an up-close and intimate perspective of the glacier, while learning about it’s recession, glacier dynamics, and the breathtaking ice features that unfold from the edge. The Mendenhall Glacier is constantly changing and offers a unique experience each day.
highlights: Canoe paddle: Paddle past icebergs, waterfalls and arctic tern nesting cliffs.
Glacier Views and Hike: Step out of the canoe and hike over the rock terrain carved out by the glacier.
Mendenhall Glacier: Get an up close and intimate perspective of the glacier from the edge, all while learning about the recession of the glacier and the wilderness that surrounds it.
Snacks: Enjoy a tasty light snack of trail mix, nuts, cheese and granola bar.

products.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Bennett Lake and Yukon Adventure
products.description: Experience the breathtaking beauty of the North as you travel into the Yukon on this historic railway & motorcoach adventure. Begin your day by traveling via motorcoach to the White Pass Train Depot where you will board the train for the 67-mile journey into the Yukon. Take the unforgettable journey aboard the "Scenic Railway of the World" as you travel from tidewater to the summit of White Pass, a 2,865-foot elevation gain, and onto beautiful Bennett Lake. Relax in a vintage railcar as you pass Bridal Veil Falls, Inspiration Point and Dead Horse Gulch --This fully narrated tour features panoramic views of mountains, gorges, waterfalls, tunnels, trestles, and historic sites. Accessible only by train, Bennett is the site of an abandoned gold rush town located at the end of the Chilkoot Trail on the shores of Bennett Lake. On arrival in Bennett, step off the train and journey back in time as you explore the White Pass historic interpretive center or take a self-guided walking tour up to the church, the views are stunning. Re-board the train and enjoy a hearty box lunch as you travel along the shores of the lake and onto the village of Carcross. Once in Carcross, you will have time to visit the charming community prior to boarding your motorcoach for the trip down the Klondike Highway and back to your ship in Skagway.
highlights: Relax in a vintage railcar along White Pass & Yukon Route Railway, offering stunning window seat views.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Yukon Adventure and White Pass Scenic Railway
products.description: Delight in Skagway’s fascinating history and spectacular mountainous scenery as you travel to the White Pass Summit and onward to the border of the Yukon territory. Next, arrive at Fraser, British Columbia, and board a motor coach to continue north through Tutshi Canyon to the border of Yukon territory. Stretch your legs with a photo stop at the "Welcome to Yukon" sign before reboarding your motorcoach.Next arrive at the Yukon Suspension Bridge where you will have time to explore on your own, enjoy a hearty bowl of bison chili and take an optional jaunt across the thrilling suspension bridge for which the venue is named. products.title: Hop-On Hop-Off City Sightseeing
products.description: Tap into your inner explorer with this British Columbia shore excursion, as you hop aboard a double-decker bus and explore Victoria's charming landmarks at your leisure.
highlights: Board your double-decker bus and begin your exploration of Victoria - with four stops to choose from and informative audio commentary, the magic of this capital city is your to discover.

Hop off at Cook Street Village, a charming shopping district where you can stroll along tree-lined streets, browse bustling boutiques and take in the relaxed atmosphere at one of the area's many cozy cafe's.

Feeling hungry? Stop at Victoria's Chinatown, the oldest in the counter, and wander the bustling neighborhood, as it comes alive with traditional Chinese architecture and the delicious aroma of fresh Asian cuisine.

Eager for a bit of culture? Pause at the iconic Empress Hotel, a luxurious four-star hotel with chateau-style architecture and a long history of distinguished guests. Just across the street, take in the beauty of the Inner Harbour, a scenic patch of waterfront.
Even more picturesque waterfront views await at the Fisherman's Wharf, which features lively restaurants, brightly-colored floating houses and a unique glimpse into Victoria's maritime heritage.

Once you've satisfied your curiosity, feel free to stay downtown and discover the capital city even further, or bring your Sightseeing City excursion to a close with a bus ride back to you awaiting ship.

products.title: Small Group: Rainforest Canopy and Zipline Adventure
products.description: Glide through the top of a rainforest canopy along a series of cables suspended between tall hemlock, spruce, and cedars and gain a unique perspective on the rainforest surrounds. This adventure excursion combines a flight over 2400 feet  of high tension cable, guaranteed to thrill all.
highlights: Depart the pier for the brief transfer to Herring Cove, located within the borders of the magnificent Tongass National Forest. This major rainforest reserve embraces one of Alaska's richest salmon spawning streams and a pristine inter-tidal estuary. Abundant wildlife habitat here includes a high concentration of bald eagles, black bear, and a variety of other species.
At Herring Cove, an all-terrain custom 4x4 vehicle transports you up a steep rain forest hillside to the canopy chalet.

Professionally trained guides outfit you with state-of-the-art safety equipment and provide a detailed orientation before you mount the first platform. Your rainforest canopy challenge features a flight over nine ziplines, an aerial bridge suspended between tall spruce, hemlock and cedar trees, and ground-based nature trails.
Your guide will progressively ease you into the forest canopy along cables ranging from a short 100 feet up to 600 feet with platform as high as 100 feet above the ground.
Eagles and bears are often seen from the platforms.
Cross a hanging bridge that will lead you to our longest zip that breaks out into the canopy offering breathtaking views of the forest and salmon spawning creek below.
Throughout this adventure, you will be surrounded by the sights and sounds of the rain forest wildlife preserve.

The final zip-line emerges from the rain forest and transports you over lush grassy wetlands to a viewing tower overlooking the Whitman Lake Fish Hatchery, 55 feet above the ground where you will gently descend to the forest floor.

Time permitting, grab a freshly brewed coffee or a cold Alaskan beer and explore our Tsimshian totem park, see a master carver working, check out our historic sawmill and watch our local blacksmiths at work.
Stop by the General Store to see photos taken during your zip-line experience and to shop for mementos of your Alaska adventure.
Your excursion concludes with a scenic drive back to the ship.

products.description: Immerse yourself in the rich flavors and traditions of Alaska with a hands-on wild salmon cooking shore excursion along the picturesque Tongass Narrows.
highlights: Begin your culinary journey with a short walk to The Local Table – a cozy venue overlooking the serene waters of the Tongass Narrows.

After your meal, conclude your Hands on Salmon Cooking Class with a satisfied palate, a wealth of new culinary skills, and a recipe booklet to recreate the dishes at home.

products.title: Explore Wild Adventure Yukon, Sled Dogs and Scenic Highway
products.description: Embark on a thrilling adventure that combines history, stunning landscapes, and the spirit of the gold rush as you journey up the legendary White Pass Summit. Your experience begins with a comfortable, narrated motor coach ride through the charming streets of downtown Skagway, where you’ll pass historic wooden boardwalks and the preserved façades of this once-bustling frontier town. As you leave the town behind, you're driver guide will share stories of Skagway's colorful history while you retrace the steps of the brave prospectors who risked everything in search of fortune during the Klondike Gold Rush. As you climb the summit, 3,292 feet above sea level, you’ll be awe-struck by breathtaking views of the rugged landscape, sweeping vistas of snow-capped peaks and glacial-fed rivers.

Afterwards, relax on a narrated drive that winds along the Ketchikan waterfront, and showcases the salmon canneries along the North Tongass Highway. THIS TOUR IS OPERATED BY A TOUR OPERATOR THAT HAS BEEN THIRD-PARTY VERIFIED TO AN INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED SUSTAINABILITY STANDARD.

products.title: Small Group: Hunting for Halibut
products.description: No matter your skill level, you will fish like a local, working the sandy flats or sea floor pinnacles with your rod in hand, drifting with the current and jigging in waters as deep as 300 feet. Species availability changes throughout the season, and daily factors such as tides and wind affect your fishing opportunities. This multi-species Alaskan fishing expedition allows you to head out with the confidence that you will catch fish. Down there in the twilight of the deep, rockfish, cod, flounder, sharks, skates, and mighty halibut find their way to your bait. No matter what the conditions or the catch, this trip offers the best chance of exposing you to the strange and alien species found on the Southeast Alaskan deep-sea floor. Historically 50% of our guests catch Pacific Halibut. 2026 updated regulations: Daily bag limit of one halibut per guest. Max lower size limit of 34”. As well, there is an upper limit fixed at 80” or greater for all 2026 dates.
highlights: Deliver your halibut and a purchase of local Alaskan seafood shipment to your home for an additional charge.
Pursue the elusive halibut with an experienced guide while learning the secrets of working the tides.
Board a Raider Pro Fisherman and experience an anglers dream come true - fishing for Pacific halibut in Alaska.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Lighthouse, Totems and Eagles
products.description: Your Alaskan captain and their crew will share Southeast Alaska's coastal history, Native culture, and natural beauty as you journey into Ketchikan's wild seascape on a wildlife-viewing cruise, with guaranteed sightings of American bald eagles. Discover the beauty of the Tongass National Forest, the world's largest temperate rainforest, and learn about Alaska's interconnected ecosystems and the animals that call this region home. Enjoy panoramic views of Totem Bight State Park from the water, including an Alaska Native clan house and a collection of 14 towering Tlingit and Haida hand-carved cedar totem poles. Breathe in the fresh sea air as you cruise to Guard Island, where you'll have the opportunity to photograph the first lighthouse to be built in Alaska, completed in 1904 and still in operation today. Sip a hot chocolate and keep on the lookout for harbor seals, moon jellyfish, pigeon guillemots, deer, blue heron, whales, and other wildlife.

products.description: Eat your way through Juneau and you'll come to know Alaska's capital city in a new and meaningful way. Your culinary host, who is a local resident, will share the best of Alaskan food, as well as local legend and lore, on this leisurely walking tour through the culinary heart of the city. Stops include award-winning restaurants and you will often meet the chefs and restaurant owners who are making Juneau a sought-after dining destination. Your menu includes king crab bisque, fresh salmon, Alaskan game, the famous hog wing, and tea from indigenous plants, earning the "Made in Alaska" seal of approval. Your experience wraps up at an historic bar, where you will sample three beers from a local brewery. This experience focuses on connecting you with the city and with Alaska, and you're sure to come away with a deeper understanding and tasty memories of your time in Juneau.

products.title: Small Group: Exclusive Alaskan Whale Discovery
products.description: It's a seafaring safari! Watch humpback whales, Steller sea lions, Dall's porpoise, orcas and harbor seals up close on this small group experience. A safari vessel, low to the water for a favorable vantage point, hosts fully guided, small-group whale-watching adventures on a voyage through Stephens Passage, a waterway known for plentiful whale activity. A professional naturalist guide leads the group through hands-on activities that support research into the entire ecosystem, such as monitoring for invasive-species, humpback-whale identification and marine mammal observations. Pull a crab pot with your guide to explore and learn more about the critters roaming around Juneau’s Ocean floor as this data is used to give back to local research and conservation efforts. The time in Stephens Passage provides a transformative opportunity not only to see whales and enjoy their antics, but to deepen one's understanding of delicate marine environments and the creatures that exist in them.
highlights: Become a Citizen Scientist by assisting in important data collecting tasks such as a crab pot pull to monitor Juneau's ocean floor for invasive species

products.title: Destination Highlight: A White Pass Scenic Railway
products.description: Sit back and take in the vast beauty of Alaska aboard what is arguably the most scenic railway in the world. The narrow-gauge White Pass & Yukon Railway is a monumental feat of engineering built in 1898 and listed among the International Historic Civil Engineering Landmarks. You’ll board a vintage railcar that’s been retrofitted for sightseeing comfort and begin a 20-mile journey from the tidewater up an incredible 2,865 feet to the summit of White Pass. Outside your window, witness the original “Trail of 98”, where brave stampeders trekked during the Klondike Gold Rush. Relish in the natural splendor of the vast wilderness as you journey through a landscape of sky-high trestles, plummeting gorges, and expansive valleys. Marvel at the beauty of Bridal Veil Falls and a breathtaking panorama, aptly named, Inspiration Point. Step back in time and rumble into unspoiled Alaska aboard the White Pass & Yukon Railroad.
highlights: Climb aboard the White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, built in 1898.
Sit back in a restored vintage railcar as you wind through the majestic Alaskan wilderness.
Follow the same route take by stampeders during the legendary Klondike Gold Rush.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Knudson Cove Salmon Fishing
products.description: Don't miss this highlight of your Alaskan Cruise - fishing for salmon. Experience a narrated tour of the city and outlying areas, learning about the history and lifestyle of Ketchikan from a seasoned and knowledgeable charter Captain. What better way to spend your time in the 'Salmon Capital of the World'? The Knudson Cove Marina welcomes the opportunity to show you, your family and friends the charm and majestic beauty of Southeast Alaska. Located just 30 minutes from downtown Ketchikan, the marina boasts a locale on prime fishing grounds. You will have about 3 hours of fishing, and due to its convenient location, you'll only be a few minutes from the dock before your lines hit the water. With only 4 to 6 passengers assigned to each fishing boat, this fleet offers you the chance for individualized attention. Whether you're an experienced angler or a novice, your Coast Guard licensed Captain will treat you to a memorable excursion as he shows you the 'ins' and 'outs' of fishing in Alaska. In between reeling in your catch, you can marvel at the wildlife – seals, sea lions, otter, porpoise, and even whales might treat you to a show, while bald eagles will offer you a superb photo opportunity as they swoop down to grasp their own "catch of the day." The type of fishing that you'll be doing is trolling for salmon with downriggers. Downriggers are used to hold the fishing lines deep in the water. Most of the time 4 to 5 rods will be used and boats hold 4-6 persons. The fishing lines will be clipped or connected to the downrigger system and then the lines are dropped to a specified depth using a large weight that is attached to the downrigger. The poles will be set or held in rod holders while the boat trolls or moves along. When a fish strikes, the line will come free of the downrigger the guest will then take the rod out of the rod holder, set the hook and play the fish. After a fish is played and landed guests will be asked to rotate over one position, sometimes one pole and type of lure catches the majority of the fish and by rotating everyone then has a fair chance of catching. To ship the daily catch home - Custom Fish Processing is available for an additional charge. Major credit card required.
highlights: Embark on a guided fishing charter into the fertile waters of Knudson Cove.
Set out your lines and get ready to real in your fresh salmon catch.
Keep your eyes peeled for seals, whales, bald eagles and other Alaskan wildlife.

Relax in these magnificent natural surroundings while you feast on wild-caught grilled Alaskan salmon, barbecue ribs, reindeer sausage, numerous side dishes, salads, and dessert, at this all-you-can-eat buffet. products.title: Destination Highlight: Endicott Arm Fjord and Glacier Explorer
products.description: Your adventure begins when you board a high-speed expedition vessel directly from your cruise ship. Relax in the warm, spacious cabin, surrounded by large windows as your experienced captain guides the vessel past shimmering icebergs. The singular maneuverability of this vessel allows you to get as close as possible to the ever-changing face of the Dawes Glacier. From the topside observation deck, absorb this unforgettable experience through all your senses. You can sometimes even feel the "white thunder" as immense spires of ice break from the glacier and crash into the sea. While exploring the length of this ice-carved fjord, you will cruise beneath cliffs rising high above the jade-colored waters. As occasions arise, the captain will stop the vessel to allow for viewing and photography of wildlife, such as pigeon guillemots, bald eagles, harbor seals, mountain goats, and bears. Departing Endicott Arm, the onboard naturalist will provide a wealth of information about the area, which naturalist John Muir called a "wild, untamed Yosemite."
highlights: Live your Alaskan dreams on this up-close exploration of spectacular Endicott Arm, including exciting views of the impressive Dawes Glacier.

products.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railway and Klondike Highway Journey
products.description: Step back in time to relive the glory days of Alaska's Gold Rush as you board a vintage rail car and retrace the original route up to the Yukon's White Pass Summit. The world-famous narrow gauge White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, revered as the "Scenic Railway of the World ", promises an unforgettable ride along the eastern side of the historic White Pass as you retrace the steps of early gold seekers, venturing along an iron trail that was built over 100 years ago through some of the North's most rugged terrain. Venture 20 miles toward the impressive 2,865-foot summit, enjoying views of Alaska's lush and verdant landscape along the way. Upon arrival at the summit, you'll board a motor coach for a narrated tour back to Skagway. Soak in the marvelous scenery and legendary landmarks as you pass by the historical areas of Brackett Wagon Road, Pitch Fork Falls, Dead Horse Gulch, and the infamous Tormented Valley. Throughout the journey, your guide will regale you with stories of the hardships faced by the stampeders, and how they overcame their struggles against the harsh climate and topography of the region.
highlights: Experience the beauty of the Yukon’s White Pass Summit by both train and motor coach.
Relive the history of Alaska’s Gold Rush era, following in the footsteps of the early gold seekers, as you ride the White Pass Railway and climb the same route as Gold Rush Stampeders.
Journey 20 miles from tidewater to the summit of the White Pass - a 2,865-foot elevation.
Soak in the magnificent panorama of mountains, gorges, waterfalls, tunnels, trestles, and historic sites from the comfort of a vintage railcar.
Learn about the significance of the White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, built in 1898 during the Klondike Gold Rush and regarded a Historic Civil Engineering Landmark.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Rainforest Canoe and Nature Trail
products.description: Today's adventure begins with a scenic drive to a secluded mountain lake, where a 37-foot Native American-style canoe - fast, stable and easy to maneuver - awaits. As you paddle under the direction of an experienced guide, scan the shore for wildlife and learn about the natural and native history of the area. The lake is surrounded by the pristine Tongass National Forest, the largest national forest in the United States and the world's largest temperate rainforest. It's the ideal place to paddle a canoe and let the stillness of nature envelope you. Step ashore at a remote base camp and enjoy an Alaska-style snack of homemade smoked salmon spread, clam chowder, rolls and wild-berry jam before following your knowledgeable guide on a short nature walk. The guide will point out the unusual flora and fauna of the old-growth forest, including a carnivorous plant, the sundew, that uses tentacles with adhesive "dew " to capture small insects. Don't miss this rare opportunity to comfortably explore Southeast Alaska the same way it was discovered by its original explorers, in harmony with nature and in tune with the haunting beauty of wild Alaska.
highlights: Fall under the spell of the peace, stillness and unparalleled splendor of Tongass National Forest as you paddle across a serene lake and walk among the towering trees.
Paddle a native canoe to experience these surroundings as pioneers did.
Learn about the plants and animals that inhibit this old-growth forest.

products.title: Small Group: Alaska's Whales, Glaciers and Rainforest Walk
products.description: Delve into the wonders of Juneau's wilderness as your professional interpretive guide leads you through the temperate rainforest surrounding the Mendenhall Glacier. Mendenhall, one of the most spectacular - yet accessible - glaciers in North America, is just 13 miles from downtown Juneau. A half-mile wide, it's also part of a 13-mile Juneau Ice Field, which terminates in Mendenhall Lake, where blue icebergs bob in the shadow of the stately Coast Mountains. The glacier is surrounded by trails of varying distances, from which you can gaze at salmon streams or marvel at tumbling waterfalls. Black bears, porcupines, beavers, and bald eagles inhabit the area around the trails, and sightings are likely. Then, take to the water in a vessel designed for marine viewing, with open stern and bow, large panel windows, and a low-to-the-water vantage point. The local captain and professional naturalist intimately know these waters and the humpback whales, orcas, and other sea creatures that inhabit them, and excel at spotting them while keeping a respectful distance. This tour unveils all the natural wonders that Juneau and the Alaskan wilderness have to offer, in a personable setting that engages both the seasoned and the novice traveler.
highlights: Gaze at the majestic Coast Mountains reflected in the calm waters of Mendenhall Lake.
Join your professional naturalist and seasoned captain as they share their local expertise and deepen your connection to Juneau.
Stretch your legs and expand your knowledge of Alaskan wildlife on a gentle hike around the Mendenhall Glacier recreational area, one of the continent's most accessible glaciers.

products.title: Destination Highlight: Musher's Camp and Sled Dog Experience
products.description: Your adventure begins with a fascinating historic tour led by your knowledgeable guide through downtown Skagway and along nine miles of breathtaking coastline to our Musher’s Camp in Dyea. From there, you will embark on an exciting ride up a rugged mountain road in our specialized unimog, surrounded by stunning glacial streams and waterfalls. Upon reaching the top, you will be greeted by your musher and a team of energetic Alaskan Huskies, ready to take you on an exhilarating ride down a mile-long trail deep within the Tongass National Rainforest on a custom-designed, wheeled sled. After your thrilling ride, your unimog will take you to a breathtaking overlook of the Dyea tidal flats, surrounded by stunning scenery. Next, enjoy a "Kennel Talk," where we will share fascinating information about dog sledding and answer any questions you may have. You'll also have plenty of time to play with our adorable puppies, whose socialization is essential to their racing success. Finally, your visit will be completed with a cozy fire and a unique gift shop.

See Alaska's awe-inspiring beauty with a private overlook of the beautiful Dyea Valley and Lynn Canal.

Along the way, pass by the infamous Gold Creek and listen to your knowledgeable driver talk about the history of the capital.

Once you reach the glacier, enjoy free time to soak in the beauty of this natural wonder; walk along the trails and keep your camera at the ready to capture unobstructed views of the river of ice. products.title: Small Group: Fly-in Norris Glacier Hike and Packraft
products.description: Adventure seekers, fly to Norris Glacier to packraft in the frigid waters of the Last Frontier before strapping on your micro-spikes for some glacier hiking as you explore this uniquely gorgeous site's mesmerizing, moonscape-like surface.
highlights: Board a six-passenger floatplane and soar through the skies of Alaska, enjoying awe-inspiring views as you glide toward the mouth of Taku Inlet, the gateway to the largest flowing glaciers in the Juneau Icefield.
Touch down on a glacial lake flanked by two magnificent glaciers, Norris and Taku, and begin your guided hike to "Packraft Cove" as you learn about packrafting's unique and thrilling charms.

Hop into a one-person packraft, an inflatable boat light that allows for deft navigation, and take to the waters, paddling past icebergs and immersing yourself in the pristine surroundings as you make your way towards the terminus of the glacier.
Trade your paddles for micro-spike crampons and a trekking pole to hike Norris Glacier and experience the unique sensation of walking on glacial ice.
After completing the glacier hike, paddle back to the cove, enjoying one final glimpse of the Alaska wild before boarding the floatplane to fly back to Juneau.

products.title: Small Group: Chilkoot Canoe Wildlife Safari
products.description: Glide into the rugged natural beauty of Alaska with this immersive shore excursion, as you hop into a canoe and set off from the town of Haines in search of the perfect blend of history, wildlife, and stunning landscapes.
highlights: Your day begins at the port in Skagway, where you enjoy a memorable ferry ride to Haines. Along the way, a knowledgeable onboard naturalist points out wildlife and discusses the geography, history and people of Southeast Alaska.

Upon your arrival in Haines, board a bus for a brief exploration of this charming town. Known as the "Adventure Capital of Alaska," Haines is steeped in history and surrounded by stunning natural beauty.

After your scenic drive along Lutak Inlet and into the river valley, arrive at tranquil Chilkoot Lake, framed by majestic mountains and dense forests. Following a safety briefing and some paddling instruction, gear up with a personal flotation device and board a Voyageur canoe, modeled after those used by gold miners and trappers in the late 1800s.
Set out across the serene waters, either paddling or letting the motor assist, as you explore the lake’s hidden inlets, coves, and salmon spawning streams.

After snapping a few post-worthy pictures, return to Haines for a relaxing picnic-style lunch at a scenic oceanfront location. Savor a selection of fresh, local fare and admire the panoramic views of the surrounding mountains, before another ferry ride brings your Chilkoot Canoe Wildlife Safari excursion to a conclusion.

products.title: Space Needle, Pike Place Market & City Tour-Airport
products.description: The “Emerald City” shines with energy, culture, and spectacular scenery — and this post-cruise shore excursion is the perfect way to experience its highlights before you head home.
highlights: Your journey begins as you board a comfortable motor coach with your knowledgeable local guide, who brings the city’s past and present to life through engaging stories and fun facts.
The tour kicks off with a drive through downtown Seattle, where modern glass towers rise alongside the city’s bustling sports stadiums, home to the Mariners, Seahawks, and Sounders.

The route continues through the International District, also known as Seattle’s Chinatown, a vibrant neighborhood where the aromas of dim sum and pho mingle with the sounds of traditional music and the chatter of locals.

Next, step back in time in Pioneer Square, the birthplace of modern Seattle. After the Great Fire of 1889, the area was rebuilt with stately brick architecture that still stands today, making it a national historic district.
The tour then takes you to Seattle’s most famous landmark – the Space Needle. Built for the 1962 World’s Fair, this futuristic structure has become an enduring symbol of innovation.
In just 43 seconds, a high-speed elevator whisks you 520 feet into the sky to the observation deck, where you’re greeted by a 360-degree panorama of the city.
Back at street level, time to dive into Seattle’s heart and soul — the Pike Place Market.

Open since 1907, this vibrant, nine-acre public market buzzes with life. Watch fishmongers hurl salmon through the air in their famous fish toss, browse artisan stalls filled with handmade crafts and local produce, and sample fresh seafood, flowers, and pastries.
Don’t miss a stop at the original Starbucks, where you can sip a cappuccino and take in the birthplace of the global coffee culture.

Enjoy some free time to wander, shop, and soak up the market’s energy, before heading to Seattle-Tacoma International Airport and concluding your Space Needle, Pike Palace Market, and City Tour excursion.

Throughout your journey, your private guide will highlight all the scenic viewpoints and regale you with the incredible story of the railroad’s beginnings. The White Pass Legacy Club is the ideal way to discover legendary White Pass, with a modern luxury twist.
highlights: Sit back and relax as you travel across Southeast Alaska in your luxury parlor car, enjoying delicious appetizers and all-inclusive beverages along the way.
Join an intimate group of travelers for an exclusive rail experience aboard the legendary White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad’s luxurious Legacy Club Car, complete with your own private guide.

Soak in spectacular views of Alaska’s iconic wilderness as you ascend to the White Pass Summit, passing by breathtaking mountains, gorges, waterfalls, tunnels, trestles, and historic sites throughout your journey.
Learn about the impressive history of the railway, built in 1898 during the Klondike Gold Rush and regarded a Historic Civil Engineering Landmark.

products.title: Small Group: Interactive Self-Guided Jeep Tour 4-Door
products.description: Embark on a self-guided journey through Juneau's stunning landscapes, all from the comfort and freedom of your own Jeep, as you capture the awe-inspiring beauty of Mendenhall Glacier or savor the tranquility of Eagle Beach.
highlights: Indulge in the freedom of your own four-door Jeep as you venture to Mendenhall Glacier, a magnificent 13-mile expanse of shimmering blue ice nestled against the backdrop of rugged mountains.
Marvel at the glacier's grandeur and witness its dynamic beauty, as it flows into Mendenhall Lake, creating a mesmerizing spectacle of nature's power and grace.
After, embark on a picturesque drive along the Alaska coast to reach the serene Shrine of St. Therese, nestled on a small island surrounded by pristine wilderness.
Discover the tranquility of this sacred site, where a quaint cobblestone church stands amidst towering trees, and keep an eye out for majestic humpback whales feeding in the surrounding waters.

Pause at Eagle Beach, a tranquil oasis where you can unwind at a scenic picnic table and take in the panoramic views of the Chilkat Mountains, while the gentle waters of the Eagle River meander beside you - the perfect backdrop for relaxation and reflection.
Next, traverse the scenic roads of Douglas Island, where breathtaking vistas of Mendenhall Glacier unfold before you, before making your way back to port.

Based on weather and seasonal factors, your guide will choose a fourth location that might include Douglas Island, DiPAC or one of several Juneau highlights. Guests are welcome to choose their own itinerary that the guide will program prior to their departure.

products.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railway and Skagway Highlights
products.description: Return to the rough-and-tumble years of the Klondike Gold Rush and ride through breathtaking scenery on this panoramic excursion. You’ll begin by boarding a vintage railcar of the famed White Pass and Yukon Route Railway, a narrow-guide railroad that once carried gold seekers hoping to strike it rich. Settle into a comfy seat, a modern new addition, and rumble through Alaska’s evergreen forests in the shadows of soaring snow-capped mountains. Marvel at the unspoiled vistas on your outbound journey that crosses over the Canadian border. Transfer to a motor coach for a return trip that showcases natural wonders and remnants of the Gold Rush era. Stop at the Historic Gold Rush Cemetery on your return to hear stories of the past and learn about the colorful characters buried there. You’ll also pass Dead Horse Gulch, where nearly 3,000 overloaded and overworked pack animals died as fevered stampeders rushed forth to stake their claims.
highlights: Take an unforgettable train-ride along the eastern side of the White Pass.
Enjoy Alaskan Gold Rush stories as you follow in their footsteps on their original route.
Spend some leisure time exploring the historic town of Skagway before heading back to the ship.
Stop at the Historic Gold Rush Cemetery on your return to hear stories of the past and learn about the colorful characters buried there.

products.title: Small Group: Norris Glacier by Helicopter and Dog Sledding
products.description: Take to the skies above Juneau aboard a helicopter and fly off to a dog sledding experience on the Norris Glacier. Soar above the Juneau Icefield and enjoy a bird’s-eye view of historic gold mines, alpine lakes, and even your own cruise ship. After a bit of flightseeing, zoom off to the Norris Glacier. Marvel as your pilot touches down on the snowy surface and step out onto an otherworldly landscape. The Norris Glacier is home to a remote camp, only accessible via helicopter, that’s manned by veterans of the legendary Alaskan Iditarod Sled Dog Race. You’ll meet up with these experts for an interactive dog sledding experience. Master the all-important commands of Hike!, Gee! And Haw! Then, feel the thrilling sensation of skidding across the snow covered terrain pulled by a team of Alaskan Sled Dogs. After your adventure, take to the air once again for the panoramic flight home.

products.description: Whether you're an experienced angler or a first-time fisherman, this shore excursion offers an unforgettable opportunity to reel in the legendary Alaskan Salmon, while enjoying first-class service, gourmet snacks, and stunning views of Alaska’s rugged wilderness.

Once aboard, meet your experienced fishing guide, who conducts a safety briefing and assists you in obtaining your sport fishing license and King Salmon stamp.

With hot coffee or a refreshing beverage in hand, settle in for a scenic cruise to the prime fishing grounds. Upon your arrival, your guide provides a fishing demonstration, teaching you the best techniques to hook and reel in the prized King Salmon.
As you fish, enjoy a selection of gourmet snacks, including charcuterie boards with cheeses, deli meats, fresh fruit, and trail mix, as well as an assortment of cookies and chips.
Once you’ve made your catch, you have the option to have it professionally processed, portioned, frozen, and even smoked in true Alaskan style, before being shipped directly to your home.
After an exciting day on the water, the boat returns to port, where your guide assists in capturing memorable photos of your successful Deluxe Salmon Fishing Excursion.

products.title: Mahoney Lake UTV Offroad Safari
products.description: Get ready to rev your engine and experience Alaska like never before on this thrilling off-road UTV shore excursion through the rugged wilderness of the Tongass National Forest - America’s largest temperate rainforest.
highlights: Your journey begins at port, where private transportation takes you along a scenic route toward the White River base camp.
Upon arrival, meet your UTV leader, gear up, and receive a quick orientation before hopping into your off-road vehicle.

With seatbelts fastened and cameras ready, set off in single-file formation, following your guide through five miles of rugged mountain trails that wind through the George Inlet Fjords and into the heart of the Tongass.
At the end of the trail lies Mahoney Lake, a secluded mountain gem fed by the cascading waters of Mahoney Falls.

After exploring and taking in the peaceful wilderness, time to refuel with a hearty outdoor lunch back at base camp, featuring a hot smoked reindeer link on a bun or a steaming cup of chili - paired with coffee, tea, or water.
Satisfied and smiling, re-board your vehicle for the scenic return to your ship—your heart still racing from your unforgettable Mahoney Lake UTV Offroad Safari excursion.

products.title: Underground Gold Mine and Panning Tour
products.description: Journey deep into Juneau’s mining history on this immersive shore excursion through the Alaska-Juneau Gold Mine, once part of the most productive gold-milling operation on Earth.
highlights: Your tour begins with a narrated drive from port along the picturesque Gastineau Channel, setting the scene for the mining story ahead.

Upon arrival at the Alaska Juneau Mine, gear up in a hard hat before following your experienced guide 360 feet into the mountain, where cool air and dim tunnels instantly transport you back to the early 1900s.
Inside the mine, learn about hard-rock mining techniques, see historic equipment come to life, and hear how miners labored in these rugged underground passages.
Above ground, explore the atmospheric ruins of the Gastineau Mill—opened in 1915 and once the world’s largest producing gold mill—and view the first steam locomotive ever brought to Alaska.

Feel the thunder of the John Peterson Stamp Mill, as it demonstrates how ore was crushed during the gold-mining boom. Then, try your hand at gold panning, with guaranteed “pay dirt” and the thrill of keeping any gold you uncover.

Before ending your Underground Gold Mine and Panning Tour excursion, browse historic relics and antiques displayed throughout the site, enjoy a complimentary hot beverage, and visit the General Store for unique souvenirs that honor Juneau’s golden legacy."

products.title: Alaskan Sourdough History & Brick Oven Pizza Making
products.description: From learning the age-old tradition of crafting sourdough starter to topping your own personal pizza, this shore excursion celebrates the region’s flavors in the most interactive way possible.
highlights: Your journey begins as you arrive at Eagles Nest and are welcomed into the warm, wood-fired aroma of the brick oven kitchen.

Your guide and chef shares the fascinating history of sourdough in Alaska—from Gold Rush-era miners who relied on their cherished starters to today’s tradition of transforming that tangy dough into perfect pizza crust.

After learning how sourdough starter becomes pizza dough, roll up your sleeves and create your own personal masterpiece, choosing from familiar favorites like pepperoni, mozzarella, sausage, and peppers, or diving into local specialties, such as smoked salmon, lemon ricotta, hot honey, pickled red onions, and house-made meatballs.

Once your pizza is baked to bubbling perfection, you settle onto the balcony with a locally brewed beer, a glass of wine, or a refreshing soda, taking in lively views of historic downtown Ketchikan and its colorful wooden storefronts.
Be sure to indulge in the Cape Fox Cannoli for dessert—a crisp pastry filled with sweet cream and chocolate chips that perfectly completes the experience.

After your meal, choose to end your Alaskan Sourdough History and Brick Oven Pizza Making excursion with a convenient ride back to port, or enjoy a leisurely walk to charming Creek Street, where boardwalks, galleries, and local history await your exploration.

products.title: Ghosts & Legends of Juneau
products.description: Delve into Juneau’s supernatural past with this (allegedly) haunted shore excursion, as you embark on a guided stroll along the wooden boardwalks of Alaska’s capital, where eerie tales of centuries past blend together with scenic coastal charm.
highlights: Your foray into the supernatural begins in historic Juneau, where your guide leads you through its atmospheric downtown district.
As you walk along the charming streets and weathered boardwalks, listen to tales of ghostly apparitions and unexplained encounters, dating back to the city’s Gold Rush era.

At the historic Alaskan Hotel and Bar, the state’s oldest operating hotel, learn of the spectral “fallen woman” said to linger in its halls and the tragic sailor who leapt from a third-floor window - his presence reportedly still felt by guests.
Continue to the legendary Red Dog Saloon, a rowdy relic of frontier days where echoes of miners’ laughter and lingering spirits seem to blend.
Pass by Juneau Drug Company and the historic Silverbow Inn, each with its own whispered stories of unexplained footsteps and shadowy figures.

Along the Totem Pole Trail, your guide decodes Indigenous teachings and symbolism, sharing lore of shape-shifters, sea serpents, Thunderbirds, the elusive Hairy Man, and the mysterious Little People woven into Tlingit tradition.
The tales extend beyond town to the doomed miners of Treadwell Mine’s “Glory Hole” and the lonely beacon of Eldred Rock Lighthouse, where hapless sailors are said to haunt the icy waters.
As you conclude your Ghosts and Legends of Juneau excursion, return to port with a few spooky tales to retell and hopefully no unwanted ghouls following you back home.
